_comment: Table-mode golden for enriching a SCHEMA-ONLY replica of the public bigquery-public-data.stackoverflow dataset (OKF's stackoverflow sample). The source is large, so we replicate a coherent 7-table subset (posts_questions, posts_answers, users, votes, comments, tags, badges) as empty, descriptions-stripped tables via eval/tools/make_public_replica.py (so enrichment must come from the grounding corpus). Out-of-the-box flow: build the replica, run the agent in table mode against it grounded by eval/corpora/stackoverflow/, then score with `python -m eval --output-dir <out> --golden goldens/table_stackoverflow.json`. Scoring matches each table by NAME. This is OKF's many-independent-entities sample (multi-concept enrichment; a single docs page often describes several tables). Facts grounded in the Stack Exchange Data Dump / SEDE schema.
_dataset_doc:
Public SOURCE dataset. Build a schema-only, description-stripped 7-table replica in your project with `python eval/tools/make_public_replica.py --source bigquery-public-data.stackoverflow --dataset stackoverflow --tables posts_questions,posts_answers,users,votes,comments,tags,badges --project

`.
grounding: eval/corpora/stackoverflow (3 local markdown docs: dataset overview, posts, users/votes/comments/tags/badges) — pass as --folders to the agent.

run._doc:
Runnable CASE for `python -m eval --run --project

`: build the 7-table schema-only replica, then enrich it in table mode grounded by the local corpus. --folders is relative to agents/enrichment.

golden_facts: one row per question; id is the primary key referenced by answers, votes, and comments
owner_user_id references users.id (the asker) and tags lists the question's topic labels that correspond to rows in the tags table
accepted_answer_id references posts_answers.id (the answer the asker accepted, null if none)
score is upvotes minus downvotes; answer_count and view_count are engagement counters
one row per answer; parent_id references posts_questions.id (the question it responds to)
owner_user_id references users.id (the answerer)
score is the answer's net votes (upvotes minus downvotes)
one row per account; id is referenced by posts (owner_user_id), comments, and badges (user_id)
reputation is the user's core earned trust score, driven by the votes their posts receive
up_votes and down_votes are the counts of votes the user has cast
one row per vote on a post; post_id references a question or answer
vote_type_id encodes the kind of vote (e.g. 2 = upvote, 3 = downvote, 1 = accepted)
votes are anonymous -- the table has no user id
one row per comment; post_id references a post and user_id references users.id
carries the comment text, its score, and creation_date
one row per topic label (tag_name, e.g. python or sql)
count is the number of questions tagged with it
excerpt_post_id and wiki_post_id reference the tag's wiki excerpt and wiki posts
one row per badge award; user_id references users.id
class encodes the badge tier (1 = gold, 2 = silver, 3 = bronze)
tag_based indicates whether the badge was earned for activity on a specific tag
